title: fix: truncate VCR snapshots for binary/video responses
author: voodoohop

gist.headline: Putting the mocks on a diet
gist.blurb: We put our test snapshots on a strict 16KB diet. No more multi-megabyte mock files clogging up the repository.
gist.summary: Truncated binary and video VCR test snapshots to a maximum of 16KB to prevent massive mock files from bloating the codebase.
gist.impact: Developers will experience a cleaner repository and faster test execution without the overhead of multi-megabyte snapshot files.
gist.visual_concept: The Nomnom creature eating a massive, towering stack of paper (representing the huge 6K line snapshots) and leaving behind a tiny, neat little floppy disk. The bee mascot is watching with a clipboard, looking relieved. Setting is a cozy retro testing lab.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the round tan nomnom creature happily eating a massive, towering stack of messy code printouts, while the cute bee mascot in a tiny lab coat watches with a clipboard, looking relieved. A retro terminal nearby glows lime green (#ecf874) showing a tiny, neat floppy disk icon. Soft pastel gradient background. Chunky pixels, warm lighting, lo-fi aesthetic like A Short Hike.
